title: The Crypto Startup Trying to Put a Barrel of Oil on Blockchain
summary: The oil industry has spent more than a century pushing into new frontiers. Its engineers pulled crude from beneath deserts, oceans and frozen tundra. Its traders built markets that turned oil into the world’s most actively traded commodity.

title: Vanguards of Healthcare: Carrum Health Rewires Care Incentives
summary: “You show me the incentive and I’ll show you the behavior,” says Sach Jain, founder and CEO of Carrum Health. Jain joins Bloomberg Intelligence analyst Jonathan Palmer to explain why misaligned incentives sit at the heart of specialty care’s cost problem. title: Germany Will Buy US Tomahawk Missiles to Close Crucial Gap
summary: Germany agreed with the US to purchase long-range, subsonic Tomahawk cruise missiles to close a dangerous gap with Russia — although numbers and delivery timelines remain unclear, and Europe is developing such missiles itself.

summary: Texas is becoming a test case for one of the biggest questions facing electricity markets. As AI-driven power demand for data centers accelerates, grid operators are exploring whether large loads can do more than consume electricity – and instead become a source of flexibility when the power system is under stress. summary: AI is reshaping the global balance of power. In the debut episode of Emerging, Bloomberg’s Menaka Doshi talks to experts on how India can gain an edge despite lacking a frontier model of its own.

title: Odd Lots: Man Group’s Massive Growth in Token Spending (Podcast)
summary: We’ve gone through a number of a technological revolutions in investing, whether it was the dawn of the high frequency trading era or the introduction of robotraders. When it comes to AI, the big question that remains in the investment context is whether or not the technology will be implemented like those past tech innovations — meaning it will be integrated into the flow of the business without upending everything as we know it — or if AI will transform the very nature of investing. title: France Launches Plan to Cut Fertilizer Import Reliance
summary: France will support a €2 billion ($2.3 billion) industrial investment program to cut dependence on costly fertilizer imports in an effort to shield farmers from future supply disruptions.

title: Stocks Get Boost From Chipmakers as Oil Steadies: Markets Wrap
summary: A resurgence in technology companies powered a rebound on Wall Street, with stocks also rising as oil steadied after jumping the most since May amid fresh hostilities between the US and Iran.

title: Europe Faces Fresh Volatility After Iran Flare-Up, Officials Say
summary: Europe’s economy is looking at a fresh bout of uncertainty following Donald Trump’s declaration that the US ceasefire with Iran is over, officials from the region said.

title: Iran Rushes Out Oil Exports as Tensions With US Flare Again
summary: Iran has rushed out oil tankers carrying roughly 11 million barrels of crude in the past 24 hours as tensions flare again with the US and President Donald Trump threatens to reimpose a blockade on its ports.

title: US, Iran Trade Airstrikes as Fears Grow of a Return to War
summary: The US military attacked Iran for a second day and Tehran retaliated against American allies in the Persian Gulf, raising fears of a return to war after little progress in efforts to secure a diplomatic outcome.

title: The PWHL is Just Getting Started With Hilary Knight
summary:
Hilary Knight is at the height of her ice hockey career, but that doesn't mean she's not already thinking about what she'll do with her time in retirement. On this episode of The Deal, Knight tells Alex Rodriguez and Jason Kelly why she one day wants to own a team 

 (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Nigeria’s $22 Billion Pension Industry Eyes Infrastructure Fund
summary: Nigeria’s $22 billion pension industry is studying plans to launch a fund later this year to help Africa’s most populous nation upgrade its frayed infrastructure.

title: Carpet Firm Victoria Inks Deal With Excluded Creditors
summary: UK carpet maker Victoria Plc and a group of bondholders left behind in an earlier debt deal have struck an agreement over a balance sheet restructuring.

title: Gold Rebounds as Traders Assess Iran Strikes, Interest-Rate Path
summary: Gold found some support following three days of losses as traders weighed a resumption of fighting in the Middle East and the US Federal Reserve’s interest-rate path.

title: Oil Steadies After Rallying on Renewed US-Iran Hostilities
summary: Oil steadied after surging the most since May as renewed hostilities between the US and Iran imperiled the recovery in shipments from the Persian Gulf.

title: Apollo’s $35 Billion AI Chip Credit Deal Is Set to Begin Trading
summary: More money managers and dealers are about to get their hands on pieces of the largest private credit deal on record — when a chunk of a $35 billion financing package for Broadcom Inc. and Anthropic PBC’s AI infrastructure expansion begins trading.

title: Damaged Manhattan Tower Owner to Reconstruct 15 Floors After Evacuation
summary: The developer behind the Midtown Manhattan tower that was evacuated over structural concerns Tuesday says it has a plan to fix the issue, including reconstructing 15 floors that were added to the original building as part of its conversion into apartments.

title: Qatar Pauses Push to Ramp Up LNG After Hormuz Tanker Attack
summary: Qatar is pausing efforts to rapidly revive production at the world’s largest liquefied natural gas facility after an attack on one of its tankers in the Strait of Hormuz raised fears that transit through the crucial waterway is still too risky.

title: European Gas Prices Jump as Qatar Pauses Push to Ramp Up LNG
summary: European natural gas prices briefly jumped above €50 a megawatt-hour for the first time in a month after a report that Qatar is pausing efforts to rapidly revive production at the world’s largest liquefied natural gas facility, threatening a recovery of flows through the Strait of Hormuz.

title: US Jobless Claims Little Changed as Layoffs Remain Limited
summary: UIS initial jobless claims decreased by 2,000 to 215,000 in the week ended July 4, a period that included the Independence Day holiday. The median forecast in a Bloomberg survey of economists called for 217,000 applications. Mike McKee reports on "Bloomberg Open Interest." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Legado de Fujimori desafía ofensiva anti-China de Trump en Perú
summary: Keiko Fujimori podría ser el tipo de líder que Donald Trump desea en América Latina. Pero los profundos vínculos de Perú con China muestran lo difícil que será para Washington revertir los avances de Pekín en la región. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: US Jobless Claims Little Changed Last Week Amid Low Layoffs
summary: Applications for US unemployment benefits were little changed last week, signaling layoffs remain limited.

title: Los Angeles Grade School for Gifted Kids to Borrow $32 Million
summary: A Los Angeles grade school that requires applicants to have an IQ of 138 or higher is planning to sell $32 million of municipal bonds on Thursday.

title: Anthopic, OpenAI Should Not Be Allowed to IPO, Says Ed Zitron
summary: Ed Zitron, CEO at EZ Primary Research, said that the risk of balancing the equity market on record public offerings for AI companies that have never reported profit is a dangerous proposition and that those companies should not be allowed to IPO. Zitron, an AI skeptic, said that many of these companies will not last in the long term. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Magnificent Seven’s Weakness Is Starting to Become a Problem for Wall Street
summary: One notable group has been absent from the 2026 stock rally: the American tech giants that have charged a nearly four-year bull run.

title: Emerging Stocks Extend Drop as Oil Swings, AI Affect Sentiment
summary: Emerging-market stocks extended the week’s declines as risk appetite fluctuated. Investors are watching US-Iran tensions, moves in oil prices and the latest developments in the artificial intelligence trade.

title: Oman Tells UN It’s Against Imposing Transit Fees in Hormuz
summary: Oman told the United Nations’ shipping agency that it doesn’t support imposing transit fees in the Strait of Hormuz, an approach which might set the sultanate at odds with Iran, which is pushing to charge passing freighters for navigation.

title: German Bond Yields Dip as Traders Watch Middle East Tensions
summary: German bond yields fell after spiking on Wednesday as investors watched for further disruption in the oil market following the latest flare-up in conflict between the US and Iran.

title: US Premarket Movers for July 9, 2026
summary: S&P 500 Index futures climb 0.2% as of 7:43 a.m. in New York. The US military struck Iran for a second day and Tehran retaliated against American allies in the Persian Gulf.

title: US, Iran Trade Strikes; SK Hynix US Offering Sees Strong Investor Demand | Bloomberg Brief 07/9/2026
summary: Stocks rebound from a brief geopolitical risk-off spell and oil rally cools after the US and Iran trade airstrikes. SK Hynix's US listing is more than seven times oversubscribed, according to people familiar with the matter. Anna Rosenberg of Amundi Investment Institute discusses the fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran. Andrew Graham of Jackson Square Capital looks at the rebound in equities. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Modi Strengthens Indo-Pacific Alliances
summary: Australia has agreed to supply uranium to India after more than a decade of talks, during Prime Minister Narendra Modi's visit to Melbourne. The deal follows defense agreements in Indonesia as India deepens its Indo-Pacific partnerships. (Source: Bloomberg)
